workshop_short_name: Geometry in the Age of Data-Driven Robotics
talk_title: Debate 1: Education (claim — mathematical foundations of geometry are a top priority for robotics education today)
speaker: Seth Hutchinson, Stefano Stramigioli vs Frank Park, Noémie Jaquier
tc_relevance_reason: Meta-discussion on whether classical geometric methods (Lie groups, screw theory) should still anchor robotics curricula in an ML-dominated era. Hutchinson and Park are co-authors of the two standard robotics textbooks. Useful context for TC educators but not technical content.
workshop_tc_importance: Geometric methods are foundational to the TC: kinematics on Lie groups, screw theory for dynamics, Riemannian optimization for trajectory generation, and manifold-aware control. This workshop's four debate panels ask whether classical geometric approaches still anchor robotics in an ML-dominated era — a direct conversation about the TC's own methodological foundations and where they intersect with learned policies.

workshop_short_name: Geometry in the Age of Data-Driven Robotics
talk_title: Debate 2: Research (claim — the vast majority of interesting research questions on the role of geometry in robotics have already been answered)
speaker: Patrick Wensing, Antonio Franchi vs Andreas Müller, Georgia Chalvatzaki
speaker_affiliation: Notre Dame / Sapienza-Twente / JKU / TU Darmstadt
tc_relevance_reason: Pro/contra debate explicitly about whether geometric robotics research is exhausted. Four panelists whose work defines the TC's geometric methods (Wensing on whole-body dynamics, Franchi on aerial control, Müller on screw theory, Chalvatzaki bridging classical and learning) arguing about the future of TC's own foundations. Genuinely once-per-conference format.
workshop_tc_importance: Geometric methods are foundational to the TC: kinematics on Lie groups, screw theory for dynamics, Riemannian optimization for trajectory generation, and manifold-aware control. This workshop's four debate panels ask whether classical geometric approaches still anchor robotics in an ML-dominated era — a direct conversation about the TC's own methodological foundations and where they intersect with learned policies.

workshop_short_name: Geometry in the Age of Data-Driven Robotics
talk_title: Debate 3: Communication (claim — formal mathematical jargon only serves to increase the field's barrier to entry)
speaker: Ross Hatton, Zachary Manchester vs Nadia Figueroa, Bruno Vilhena Adorno
tc_relevance_reason: Pro/contra debate on whether the math-heavy style of geometric robotics papers excludes potential contributors. Important meta-discussion but more about field culture than technical methodology.
workshop_tc_importance: Geometric methods are foundational to the TC: kinematics on Lie groups, screw theory for dynamics, Riemannian optimization for trajectory generation, and manifold-aware control. This workshop's four debate panels ask whether classical geometric approaches still anchor robotics in an ML-dominated era — a direct conversation about the TC's own methodological foundations and where they intersect with learned policies.

workshop_short_name: Geometry in the Age of Data-Driven Robotics
talk_title: Full Panel Discussion (synthesis across all three debates)
speaker: Hutchinson, Stramigioli, Park, Jaquier, Wensing, Franchi, Müller, Chalvatzaki, Hatton, Manchester, Figueroa, Adorno
tc_relevance_reason: All twelve panelists in one room synthesizing the day's debates on geometric methods in robotics. Pulls together TC senior figures from kinematics (Park), dynamics (Wensing, Manchester), control (Stramigioli, Franchi), geometric mechanics (Hatton, Adorno) and learning (Jaquier, Chalvatzaki, Figueroa) at the same table. Closing community moment of the workshop day.
workshop_tc_importance: Geometric methods are foundational to the TC: kinematics on Lie groups, screw theory for dynamics, Riemannian optimization for trajectory generation, and manifold-aware control. workshop_short_name: AI-Driven Safe Aerial Robotics
speaker: Nora Ayanian
tc_relevance_reason: Collision avoidance for multiple aerial vehicles is a core motion-planning problem. Ayanian's group works on decentralized multi-robot coordination algorithms — exactly the kind of distributed planning the TC cares about.
workshop_tc_importance: Aerial robotics is a heavy consumer of TC methods: MPC and MPPI for tracking, trajectory optimization for agile flight, control barrier functions for safety, and RL-augmented controllers for envelope-edge maneuvers. The workshop's safety framing keeps the talks on the planning and control side rather than perception in isolation. Includes talks on learned MPC for aggressive flight and multi-vehicle collision avoidance.

workshop_short_name: Uncertainty in Open-World Robotics
speaker: Marija Popović
tc_relevance_reason: Popović works on informative path planning — choosing UAV trajectories that maximize information gain for environmental monitoring. Direct TC scope: planning under uncertainty with information-theoretic objectives. Talk title not yet published but her core research area is squarely TC.
workshop_tc_importance: Planning under uncertainty is a defining TC topic — POMDPs, belief-space planning, informative path planning, and risk-aware MPC all fall here. The workshop's hierarchical-planning and information-gathering talks map directly onto motion planning when the environment is partially observed or actively changing. Small but topically dense for TC scope.

workshop_tc_importance: Field robotics is a deployment lens on TC methods: trajectory optimization for field UAVs, energy-aware motion planning for AUVs, coverage planning for inspection, and LiDAR-centric navigation for outdoor robots all show up here. The morning and afternoon talks lean heavily into the planning and control side of deploying autonomous systems outside the lab.

workshop_short_name: Workshop on Field Robotics
speaker: Fu Zhang
tc_relevance_reason: Fu Zhang's group is behind FAST-LIO and FAST-LIO2 — the most widely-used LiDAR-inertial odometry stacks for aerial robots. This talk on LiDAR-centric navigation for field drones is bullseye for TC members deploying UAVs in GPS-denied outdoor settings.
